max_clique.description: Given the graph, find the maximal clique.

parameters.description: Find the largest complete subgraph where all nodes are connected to each other

standard.system_message: You are an expert in visual analysis of graphs. The input is a visual representation of a graph. Nodes are represented as yellow circles with black borders each containing a unique integer label, nodes are ONLY connected if there is a visible black straight line drawn between them, the absence of a line means there is NO connection between those nodes. Provide direct answers without intermediate steps. Always format your output as: 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'
standard.user_message: Please, find the clique of maximum size in the graph, that is the largest subset of nodes that are pairwise adjacent (every node in the subset is connected to every other node in the subset). The size of the clique is the number of nodes in the subset. Before submitting your final answer, verify that: 1) Every pair of nodes in your clique is connected by an edge in the graph, and 2) No larger subset of nodes forms a complete subgraph. Format your answer as 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'

cot.system_message: You are an expert in visual analysis of graphs. The input is a visual representation of a graph. Nodes are represented as yellow circles with black borders each containing a unique integer label, nodes are ONLY connected if there is a visible black straight line drawn between them, the absence of a line means there is NO connection between those nodes. Show your step-by-step reasoning process. Always format your output as: 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'
cot.user_message: Please, find the clique of maximum size in the graph, that is the largest subset of nodes that are pairwise adjacent (every node in the subset is connected to every other node in the subset). The size of the clique is the number of nodes in the subset. To solve this task, reason step by step by: 1) Identifying densely connected regions in the graph, 2) For promising regions, checking if all nodes in the potential clique are connected to each other, 3) Systematically searching for increasingly larger cliques, and 4) Verifying no larger clique exists. Before submitting your final answer, verify that: 1) Every pair of nodes in your clique is connected by an edge in the graph, and 2) No larger subset of nodes forms a complete subgraph. Format your final answer as 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'

soal.system_message: You are an expert in visual analysis of graphs. The input is a visual representation of a graph. Nodes are represented as yellow circles with black borders each containing a unique integer label, nodes are ONLY connected if there is a visible black straight line drawn between them, the absence of a line means there is NO connection between those nodes. Begin by constructing the adjacency list before solving the maximum clique problem. Always format your output as: 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'
soal.user_message: Please, find the clique of maximum size in the graph, that is the largest subset of nodes that are pairwise adjacent (every node in the subset is connected to every other node in the subset). The size of the clique is the number of nodes in the subset. To solve this task, first compute the adjacency list of the graph by listing all nodes and their direct connections (e.g., 'Node 1: 2,3,4' means node 1 is connected to nodes 2, 3, and 4). Then use this adjacency list together with the given visual representation to identify the maximum clique. Note that a maximum clique is likely to be found among the most densely connected regions of the graph. Before submitting your final answer, verify that: 1) Every pair of nodes in your clique is connected by an edge in the graph, and 2) No larger subset of nodes forms a complete subgraph. Format your final answer as 'Clique: [comma-separated nodes], Size: <number>'

examples.task_solution: I'll find the maximum clique by examining the graph systematically. It only makes sense to explore subsets of size at most the maximum degree of a node plus one. First, I'll look for densely connected regions. I notice that nodes 1, 2, 3, and 4 form a particularly dense subgraph. Checking each pair of connections: 1-2, 1-3, 1-4, 2-3, 2-4, and 3-4, I confirm all are connected. I've verified that nodes 1, 2, 3, and 4 form a 4-node clique. I'll check if there's any 5-node clique by trying to add another node to this set, but no other node connects to all four of these nodes. Therefore, the maximum clique is Clique: [1,2,3,4], Size: 4.

I'll search for the maximum clique by examining densely connected regions in the graph. It only makes sense to explore subsets of size at most the maximum degree of a node plus one. I notice that nodes 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10 appear to form a dense subgraph. Checking each pair of connections (10 pairs total): 5-6, 5-8, 5-9, 5-10, 6-8, 6-9, 6-10, 8-9, 8-10, and 9-10, I confirm all are connected. I've verified that nodes 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10 form a 5-node clique. I'll check if there's any 6-node clique by trying to add another node to this set, but no other node connects to all five of these nodes. Therefore, the maximum clique is Clique: [5,6,8,9,10], Size: 5.

First, I'll construct the adjacency list for the graph: 1: 2,3,4; 2: 1,3,4,5,7; 3: 1,2,4,7; 4: 1,2,3,7; 5: 2,6,7,8; 6: 5,7; 7: 2,3,4,5,6,8; 8: 5,7. Using both the adjacency list and the image, I can see that nodes 1, 2, 3, and 4 are all connected to each other, forming a 4-node clique. I've checked for larger cliques by trying to add a fifth node, but no other node is connected to all four of these nodes. Specifically, node 5 is not connected to node 1, node 6 is not connected to nodes 2 and 4, and node 7 is not connected to node 3. Therefore, the maximum clique is Clique: [1,2,3,4], Size: 4.

First, I'll construct the adjacency list for the graph: 1: 2,3,4; 2: 1,3,4,5,7; 3: 1,2; 4: 1,2,7,11; 5: 2,6,7,8,9,10; 6: 5,7,8,9,10,11;  7: 2,4,5,6,8; 8: 5,6,7,9,10; 9: 5,6,8,10; 10: 5,6,8,9; 11: 4,6. Using both the adjacency list and the image, I can identify that nodes 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10 form a complete subgraph where every node is connected to every other node in the set. I've verified all 10 possible connections exist. I've also checked that this is maximal by trying to add a 6th node, but no other node is connected to all five of these nodes. Therefore, the maximum clique is Clique: [5,6,8,9,10], Size: 5.
